title: 2,4-Dimethyl-6-hydroxyacetophenoneoxime as an Extraction Photometric Reagent for the Simultaneous Estimation of Copper and Nickel
author: M. J. EAPEN
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Inorganic Chemistry Division, National Chemical Laboratory, Poona-411 008 

  Manuscript received 3 October, 1989, revised 29 August 1990, accepted 12 October 1990  

 2,4-Dimethyl 6-hydroxyacetophenone oxime has been used as an extraction photometric reagent for the determination of microgram quantities of copper In the presence of Ni ll , Fe lll , Al III , zn II , Cr Ill  and Ph ll  by extracting Its complex using tartrate or diethylamine at pH 8.5-9.0. Microgram quantities of nickel were also determined in the presence of Fe llI , zn II , Cd II , Al lII , Cr lll  and Ph Il  using hydroxylamine hydrochloride or diethylamine. The reagent could also be used for the simultaneous extraction photometric determination of Cu II  and Ni II .
